actions.label: Cold Outreach Blitz
actions.description: Spend the month calling, emailing, and DM-ing everyone who might need what you sell. Unglamorous, but it's how first dollars get made.
actions.lesson: Early on, volume wins. Talk to enough of the right people and the math produces clients. Don't polish the funnel before you've filled it.
actions.narrative_success: Three new clients this month — none of them glamorous, all of them paying. Your phone is ringing now.
actions.narrative_failure: Forty calls, twelve conversations, one maybe. But that one maybe turned into a yes on the last day of the month.

actions.label: Ask for Referrals
actions.description: Systematically ask every past and current client for warm introductions. Costs nothing but ego.
actions.lesson: Referrals are the cheapest, highest-trust leads you'll ever get. Happy clients are a sales force you don't pay — but only if you actually ask.
actions.narrative_success: Your best client introduced you to three people at her networking group. Two of them signed by Friday.
actions.narrative_failure: Everyone said they'd think about it. One person actually came through — better than nothing.

actions.label: Local Networking
actions.description: Hit every chamber event, BNI meeting, and coffee meetup in your area. Shake hands until your palm hurts.
actions.lesson: In-person is the highest-trust, highest-conversion channel there is. People buy from people they've met — especially before you have a reputation to lean on.
actions.narrative_success: You're becoming a familiar face. Two people approached you this time instead of the other way around.
actions.narrative_failure: A lot of business cards exchanged, no deals closed. But three people now know your name.

actions.label: Social Media Content
actions.description: Post consistently about your work — case studies, tips, behind-the-scenes. Build an audience one post at a time.
actions.lesson: Organic content compounds slowly but never stops. It won't pay this month — it builds an audience asset that lowers the cost of every future customer.
actions.narrative_success: One post went semi-viral in your niche. Your DMs have actual prospects in them for the first time.
actions.narrative_failure: Consistent posting, crickets responding. But the archive is building — it'll compound eventually.

actions.label: Test Paid Ads
actions.description: Run a small Facebook or Google ads test. Burn a little cash to learn what message lands.
actions.lesson: Master one channel at a time. Paid ads give the fastest feedback and highest early conversion — pour in there first, build organic in parallel for the long game.
actions.narrative_success: The ads are profitable. Not wildly — but $500 in turned into $1,500 back. You've found a lever.
actions.narrative_failure: The $500 mostly evaporated. But you learned what doesn't work, which is worth something.

actions.label: Refine Your Offer
actions.description: Stop selling time. Package your expertise into a clear, premium offer with defined scope and pricing.
actions.lesson: Sell outcomes, not hours — to people who can pay. The fastest path to revenue is an irresistible offer that solves an expensive problem for an audience with money.
actions.narrative_success: The new pricing feels scary — and every new prospect is saying yes faster than before. You were undercharging.
actions.narrative_failure: Two clients pushed back on the new pricing. You compromised on one. The packaging needs another pass.

actions.label: Hire a Salesperson
actions.description: Bring on someone whose only job is closing deals. Your calendar stops being the bottleneck.
actions.lesson: A great product is your best salesperson. When delivery is exceptional, testimonials and referrals do the closing for you — conversion climbs and you need less sales firepower, sometimes none. Perfect the product before you scale the sales team.
actions.narrative_success: She closed three deals in her first two weeks. You spent that time on operations instead of selling — a first.
actions.narrative_failure: The new hire is ramping slowly. Revenue didn't spike yet, but you got ten hours a week back.

actions.label: Install CRM & Pipeline
actions.description: Stop tracking leads in your head. Build a real sales pipeline with stages, follow-ups, and metrics.
actions.lesson: What gets tracked gets closed. A real pipeline surfaces the leads you'd otherwise forget — most lost sales die from neglect, not rejection.
actions.narrative_success: For the first time, you can see every prospect, where they are, and what happens next. Three forgotten leads turned into deals.
actions.narrative_failure: The CRM is set up but half your data is still in sticky notes. Partial improvement — you'll migrate the rest next month.

actions.label: Launch a Webinar Funnel
actions.description: Build an automated webinar that sells while you sleep. High setup cost, compounding returns.
actions.lesson: Build it once, it sells forever. An automated funnel turns your best pitch into an asset that works 24/7 — selling without costing you time.
actions.narrative_success: The webinar converted at 8%. People are signing up, watching, and buying — at 2 AM while you're asleep.
actions.narrative_failure: Registration was decent. Conversion was terrible. The offer needs reworking, but the funnel infrastructure is built.

actions.label: Build Referral Partnerships
actions.description: Set up formal referral agreements with complementary businesses. Their clients become your leads.
actions.lesson: Borrow other people's trust. Partnering with businesses that serve your same client (but don't compete) turns their audience into your near-free lead source.
actions.narrative_success: Three partners, each sending you one to two warm leads a month. The acquisition cost on these is nearly zero.
actions.narrative_failure: One partnership is producing. The other two signed the agreement but haven't sent anyone yet. Follow up next month.

actions.label: PR & Brand Campaign
actions.description: Invest in a real brand push — podcast appearances, press features, a professional brand refresh.
actions.lesson: Authority shortens every sale. When prospects already know your name from a podcast or feature, you're not convincing them — you're confirming what they expected.
actions.narrative_success: A local business podcast featured you. Three inbound calls the next week, all mentioning the episode.
actions.narrative_failure: The rebrand looks great. The press pitch didn't land. Brand equity went up; the phone didn't ring more. Yet.

actions.label: Build a Content Engine
actions.description: Hire a content team. Blog, video, podcast — all running without you. The brand grows on autopilot.
actions.lesson: A content team makes your brand grow on autopilot. You stop trading hours for attention — the audience compounds whether you show up that day or not.
actions.narrative_success: The content calendar runs itself. You approved this month's posts in twenty minutes. Leads are up 30%.
actions.narrative_failure: The team is producing content. It's... fine. Not your voice yet. Takes time to train a brand voice into other people.

actions.label: License or Franchise
actions.description: Package your business model for others to run. Your brand, their sweat. Passive revenue at scale.
actions.lesson: Sell the system, not your time. Licensing your playbook lets others run your model — your brand, their effort, your royalty. Pure leverage.
actions.narrative_success: Two licensees signed. They're paying you monthly for the right to use your name and your playbook. This is leverage.
actions.narrative_failure: One licensee signed, then struggled. Supporting them took more time than expected. The model needs refinement.

actions.label: Acquire a Competitor
actions.description: Buy a smaller competitor's customer base and fold it into your operation. Instant market share.
actions.lesson: You can buy growth, not just earn it. Acquiring a competitor's client base is instant market share — often cheaper than winning those customers one at a time.
actions.narrative_success: The acquisition closed. Thirty new clients, a trained team, and a competitor who's no longer competing.
actions.narrative_failure: The deal closed but integration is messy. Half the acquired clients churned in the transition.

actions.label: Launch Email Nurture Campaign
actions.description: Build an email list from your leads. Weekly value emails that warm them up until they're ready to buy.
actions.lesson: An email list is an asset YOU own — not rented from an algorithm. Nurture it and you can create revenue almost on demand for years.
actions.narrative_success: The email sequence is converting. Three leads became clients this month — they said your emails made them feel like they already knew you.
actions.narrative_failure: Open rates are decent, clicks are low. The content needs work, but the infrastructure is built.

actions.label: Create a Lead Magnet
actions.description: Free guide, checklist, or mini-course that captures emails. Give value first, sell later.
actions.lesson: Give value before you ask for the sale. A free, genuinely useful resource earns trust and an email address — the start of a relationship you own.
actions.narrative_success: The lead magnet is live. Fifteen new emails in the first week. These aren't customers yet — but they raised their hand.
actions.narrative_failure: Downloads are trickling in. The topic might need adjusting, but you've got five new leads and a distribution channel.

actions.label: Build JV/Affiliate Network
actions.description: Create a network of affiliates and joint-venture partners who sell for you on commission.
actions.lesson: An affiliate network is an army selling for you on commission — you only pay for results. Other people's audiences become your growth channel, with almost no downside risk.
actions.narrative_success: Twelve affiliates are actively promoting. Revenue from channels you didn't build yourself. This is what scale feels like.
actions.narrative_failure: The network exists on paper. Three affiliates are actually producing. The rest need nurturing.
